diagrams.title: Process Structure for Critical Section
diagrams.description: Shows the four sections of a process in CS problem: Entry, Critical, Exit, and Remainder sections with flow arrows
elements: Arrows showing sequential flow between sections
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the structure of solutions to the critical section problem

diagrams.title: Peterson's Algorithm for Two Processes
diagrams.description: Diagram showing two processes (P0 and P1) with shared variables flag[0], flag[1], and turn. Shows decision flow for which process enters CS
elements: Decision diamond: if flag[other] is true and turn is mine, wait else enter
Arrow showing which process enters CS
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the Peterson's solution logic for mutual exclusion

diagrams.title: Test-and-Set Lock Operation
diagrams.description: Shows atomic Test-and-Set operation reading lock value and setting to true in single step
elements: Process enters if old value was 0
diagrams.purpose: Understanding hardware atomic instruction for mutual exclusion

diagrams.title: Three Requirements Venn Diagram
diagrams.description: Three overlapping circles showing Mutual Exclusion, Progress, and Bounded Waiting as overlapping regions representing valid solutions
elements: Center overlap: Valid CS solution region
diagrams.purpose: Understanding that ALL three requirements must be satisfied together
